title: Epilepsy Multiclassification Mobile Application
shortDescription: A research-driven mobile and backend system exploring EEG-based epilepsy multiclassification, preprocessing, model inference, and accessible result presentation.
overview: The project combines signal preprocessing, machine learning experimentation, backend services, and a mobile interface.
It explores how complex EEG classification workflows can be organized into a usable software system for analysis and presentation.
problem: EEG seizure analysis involves large datasets, complex preprocessing, multiple seizure classes, and model outputs that are difficult to present in an accessible workflow.
solution: The system architecture separates preprocessing, model execution, API services, background processing, and a React Native interface for displaying results and supporting the user journey.
results: A complete system design connecting ML and mobile development
A practical interface concept for complex model output
